name: telebot-components

metadata.name: telebot-components
metadata.summary: Framework/toolkit for building Telegram bots with telebot and redis

documentation:
# telebot-components

Framework / toolkit for building bots with [telebot](https://github.com/bots-against-war/telebot).

## Development

### Setup

1. Clone repository
   ```bash
   git clone git@github.com:bots-against-war/telebot-components.git baw
   cd ./baw
   ```

2. The project requires Poerty 1.5.1 (see [installation instruction](https://python-poetry.org/docs/master#installing-with-the-official-installer)).
   For example, to install `1.5.1` on Unix, run
   ```bash
   curl -sSL https://install.python-poetry.org | python3 - --version 1.5.1
   ```

3. Then, to install the library with all dependencies, run from project root
   ```bash
   poetry install
   ```
   - You might need to manually install dynamic versioning plugin (without it local build will
     always have version `0.0.0`):
     ```bash
     poetry self add poetry-dynamic-versioning-plugin
     ```
   - To create virtualenv inside the project’s root directory, use command
     ```bash
     poetry config virtualenvs.in-project false --local
     ```
4. Run `pre-commit` to set up git hook scripts
   ```bash
   pre-commit install
   ```


### Testing
Use command below for run tests
```bash
poetry run pytest tests -vv
```

To also generate test coverage report

```bash
poetry run coverage run -m pytest tests -vv && poetry run coverage report
```

By default, all tests are run with in-memory Redis emulation. But if you want you can run them
locally on real Redis by specifying `REDIS_URL` environment variable before running `pytest`.

> **Note**: Tests must be able to find an empty Redis DB to use; they also clean up after themselves.

### Start example bot
For first start you need to do 3 things:
1. Use command below to generate environment variables file:
    ```bash
    cp ./examples/example.env ./examples/.env
    ```
   > **Note**: After `.env` file is generated you will need to add your [bot's token](https://core.telegram.org/bots#6-botfather) to it.  
   > Also for bot with `trello integration` you need to add `trello` token and api key. You can get it [here](https://trello.com/app-key).
2. If you want start redis on local machine, run
    ```bash
    docker run --name baw-redis -d -p 6379:6379 redis redis-server --save 60 1 --loglevel warning
    ```
3. Run any bot from `./examples`
    ```bash
    python3 ./examples/feedback_bot.py  // or run with IDE from bot file
    ```

classes.name: telebot_components.stores.user_group.UserGroupStore
documentation:
Generic user group store with an arbitrary user identity function.

User identity is an async User -> str function, allowing user identification by id, username or any other attribute.

classes.name: telebot_components.stores.banned_users.BannedUsersStore

methods.name: telebot_components.stores.banned_users.BannedUsersStore.not_from_banned_user
documentation: Can be used in telebot's 'func' filter

Simple store for banned users. Implements inmemory cache: we have to check
if a user is banned far more often than we need to ban someone.

Currently only supports permanent ban.

NOTE: deprecated, for new applications use UserGroupStore

methods.name: telebot_components.stores.generic.KeyVersionedValueStore.load
documentation: KeyValueStore-compatible method, see also load_version

Key-value store for structured data with multiple versions of the value available.
Note that diffing is performed on data structure, not serialized value. So, for simple
values like strings the store just stores all version values

classes.name: telebot_components.feedback.integration.interface.FeedbackHandlerIntegration

methods.name: telebot_components.feedback.integration.interface.FeedbackHandlerIntegration.help_message_section
documentation: If this method returns non-empty string, it is added to admin chat's /help

methods.name: telebot_components.feedback.integration.interface.FeedbackHandlerIntegration.name
documentation: Human-readable integration name to display to admins

methods.name: telebot_components.feedback.integration.interface.FeedbackHandlerIntegration.handle_user_message
documentation:
The method is invoked on all user messages (including emulated) passing through the feedback handler.
- `admin_chat_message` is a message as it appears in the main admin chat, allows backlinking and other stuff
- `user` is a original message author
- `user_message` is a message as it was sent by the user; not always available (e.g. emulated messages)
- `category` is a user category, if enabled in the main admin chat
- `bot` is a bot

methods.name: telebot_components.feedback.integration.interface.FeedbackHandlerIntegration.handle_user_message_replied_elsewhere
documentation:
The method is invoked when admins have replied to user message in the main admin chat or
in other integrations (in which case even will be UserMessageRepliedFromIntegrationEvent).

methods.name: telebot_components.feedback.integration.interface.FeedbackHandlerIntegration.aux_endpoints
documentation:
Optional hook for the integration to specify aux endpoints it requires for functioning (e.g. webhook
for some external service)

methods.name: telebot_components.feedback.integration.interface.FeedbackHandlerIntegration.background_job
documentation:
Optional coroutine that should be run as a background job along with the bot.
Receives the 'server is listening' future that can be awaited if the initialization requires the
listening HTTP server (including aux_endpoints).

methods.name: telebot_components.feedback.integration.interface.FeedbackHandlerIntegration.setup
documentation: Optional hook for the integration to set up all the bot-related logic it needs (e.g. handlers)

Interface class for feedback handler integrations: components extending the default
FeedbackHandler behavior by
  - additionally handling all user messages (e.g. exporting them to some new format / medium)
  - receiving an additional admin-side input, notifying the main admin chat and other
    integrations about it

methods.name: telebot_components.feedback.integration.trello.TrelloIntegration._append_card_content
documentation:
Fetch an existing card description, add an appendix and update the description. Returns Card object.

On card overflow, create a new card with a description appendix, adding "previous card" and "next card" links.
In this case returns Card object for the new card. If some additional processing is needed on the old card,
old_trello_card_callback can be used.

classes.name: telebot_components.feedback.FeedbackHandler

methods.name: telebot_components.feedback.FeedbackHandler.user_identifier
documentation: Human readable identifier for the user (not to be confused with user id)

methods.name: telebot_components.feedback.FeedbackHandler.emulate_user_message
documentation:
Sometimes we want FeedbackHandler to act like the user has sent us a message, but without actually
a message there (they might have pressed a button or interacted with the bot in some other way). This
method can be used in such cases.

If the message has been successfully sent to the admin chat, this method returns its id.

A class incapsulating the following workflow:
 - people write messages to the bot
 - the bot forwards/copies messages to admin chat
 - admins reply
 - the bot copies messages back to the user

variables.name: telebot_components.form.types.FormFieldValueId
documentation: see FormField.value_id method

classes.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.pipeline
documentation:
Return a new pipeline object that can queue multiple commands for
later execution. `transaction` indicates whether all commands
should be executed atomically. Apart from making a group of operations
atomic, pipelines are useful for reducing the back-and-forth overhead
between the client and server.

Note that pipeline implements the same RedisInterface

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.set
documentation:
Set the value at key `name` to `value`
`ex` sets an expire flag on key `name` for `ex` seconds.
`px` sets an expire flag on key `name` for `px` milliseconds.
`nx` if set to True, set the value at key `name` to `value` only

> if it does not exist.

`xx` if set to True, set the value at key `name` to `value` only

: if it already exists.

`keepttl` if True, retain the time to live associated with the key.

: (Available since Redis 6.0)

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.get
documentation: Return the value at key `name`, or None if the key doesn't exist

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.expire
documentation:
Set an expire flag on key `name` for `time` seconds. `time`
can be represented by an integer or a Python timedelta object.

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.delete
documentation: Delete one or more keys specified by `names` and return number of deleted keys

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.copy
documentation:
Copy the value stored in the `source` key to the `destination` key.

`destination_db` an alternative destination database. By default,
the `destination` key is created in the source Redis database.

`replace` whether the `destination` key should be removed before
copying the value to it. By default, the value is not copied if
the `destination` key already exists.

For more information see <https://redis.io/commands/copy>

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.rename
documentation:
Rename key `src` to `dst`

For more information see <https://redis.io/commands/rename>

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.sadd
documentation: Add `value(s)` to set `name` and return number of values added to the set

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.srem
documentation: Remove `values` from set `name` and return number of actually removed values

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.smembers
documentation: Return all members of the set ``name``

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.sismember
documentation: Return a boolean indicating if `value` is a member of set `name`

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.spop
documentation: Remove and return a random member of set `name`, or an array of members, when count is specified

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.incr
documentation:
Increments the value of `key` by 1 and return its value after the operation.
If no key exists, the value will be initialized as 0 and then incremented.

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.rpush
documentation: Push `values` onto the tail of the list `name` and return list length after the operation

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.rpop
documentation:
Removes and returns the last elements of the list `name`.

By default, the command pops a single element from the end of the list.
When provided with the optional `count` argument, the reply will
consist of up to count elements, depending on the list's length.

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.lrange
documentation:
Return a slice of the list `name` between
position `start` and `end`
`start` and `end` can be negative numbers just like
Python slicing notation

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.llen
documentation:
Return the length of the list `name`

For more information see <https://redis.io/commands/llen>

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.lset
documentation:
Set element at `index` of list `name` to `value`

For more information see <https://redis.io/commands/lset>

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.ltrim
documentation:
Trim the list `name`, removing all values not within the slice
between `start` and `end`

`start` and `end` can be negative numbers just like
Python slicing notation

For more information see <https://redis.io/commands/ltrim>

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.exists
documentation: Returns the number of `names` that exist

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.keys
documentation: Returns a list of keys matching `pattern`. Note that keys are returned as bytes

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.hset
documentation:
Set `key` to `value` within hash `name`,
`mapping` accepts a dict of key/value pairs that will be
added to hash `name`.
`items` accepts a list of key/value pairs that will be
added to hash `name`.
Returns the number of fields that were added.

For more information see <https://redis.io/commands/hset>

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.hget
documentation: Return the value of `key` within the hash `name`

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.hkeys
documentation: Return the list of keys within hash ``name``

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.hvals
documentation: Return the list of values within hash ``name``

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.hlen
documentation:
Return the number of elements in hash `name`

For more information see <https://redis.io/commands/hlen>

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.hgetall
documentation:
Return a Python dict of the hash's name/value pairs

For more information see https://redis.io/commands/hgetall

methods.name: telebot_components.redis_utils.interface.RedisInterface.hdel
documentation: Delete `keys` from hash `name`

Abstract interface for the parts of redis.asyncio.Redis class we're using. Update when using
new methods from Redis, not listed here, and when updating Redis client library.

Note that this is an interface for Redis configured to not decode responses and return plain bytes
(i.e. it must not specify decode_responses=True option).

This is not a true typeshed because Redis library uses a lot of dynamic features and complex inheritance.
When using real Redis instance in place of RedisInterface, mypy may complain, but we have to ignore it.
type defs copied from redis

methods.name: telebot_components.form.form.Form.branching
documentation:
Branching form is a simplified way to configure form, using a generally linear flow with braches entered
with condition on previous field's value. After branch is completed, the form "merges" back and proceeds
sequentially.

methods.name: telebot_components.form.form.Form.format_graph
documentation: VVVVVV messy shitty terminal visualization code VVVVVV

Container for collection of fields linked together via get_next_field_getter() attribute. Does not modify passed
objects, creates private copies.

If allow_cyclic param is False (default), performs topological sort to validate form acyclicity and can print
it's graph structure in ASCII with print_graph method.

methods.name: telebot_components.form.field.FormField.custom_value_type
documentation:
Used for validation of a form's result type (see Form.validate_result_type). In trivial cases
like PlainTextField field value type is obtained from introspection, but sometimes this is
impossible (e.g. in MultipleSelectField), and this method is used.

If your custom FormField subclass has a complex dynamic value type, override this method and
return this type.

methods.name: telebot_components.form.field.FormField.texts
documentation:
Used for build-time validation that all the fields in the form are properly localised.

Automatically collects AnyText-typed fields using dataclass introspection. Anything else must be added
via custom_texts() hook.

methods.name: telebot_components.form.field.FormField.custom_texts

If subclasses add their own field-related texts (custom error messages,
button captions or anything else, they must list them here)

methods.name: telebot_components.form.field.FormField.with_output_opts
documentation: Typed version of dataclasses.replace

methods.name: telebot_components.form.field.AttachmentsField.process_message
documentation:
HACK: we want to process media group, but telegram passes them as separate messages,
linked only with ID with no info on the total number of items, order or whatever.

As a workaround, we use a little non-persistent cache internal to field. We store the
first message, sleep asynchronously for some time and hope that by the time we wake up,
all other messages in the media group have arrived and are already added to the cache

classes.name: telebot_components.utils.airtable.AirtableConfig
documentation:
Serialized Airtable table configuration.

column_id_by_name has the following format:
{
    "name": "fldlKmD92YqW6Pdlw",
    "age": "fldwwBJb1FSA03Yvg",
    ...
}
here "fldXXXXXXXXXXXXXX" values are provided by Airtable API docs, and "name", "age" etc must correspond
to the names of AirtableColumnEnumT implementation members

classes.name: telebot_components.utils.airtable.AirtableRecord
documentation: Utility class wrapping data returned from Airtable queries

classes.name: telebot_components.utils.airtable.AirtableApi

methods.name: telebot_components.utils.airtable.AirtableApi.create
documentation: Returning record id as a string

pyairtable wrapper:
- allows base/table configuration in a serializable format
- wraps blocking operations as asynchronous with thread pool executor
- parametrized with column name enumeration for readable queries

classes.name: telebot_components.utils.alerts.TelegramAlertsHandler
documentation: Sentry-like logging integration: report all error level logs to a dedicated alert Telegram channel

modules.name: telebot_components.utils.diff
documentation:
Diff and patch operations for list- and dict-like structures.

Heaviliy based on dictdiffer (https://github.com/inveniosoftware/dictdiffer) package,
with the main changes:
- reduced functionality we don't need
- different diff format with TypedDict-based typing
- better list diff support with difflib.SequenceMatcher
  (as proposed in https://github.com/inveniosoftware/dictdiffer/issues/100)

functions.name: telebot_components.utils.telegram_message_url
documentation:
Note: there are two ways to link to a comment to a channel post:
1. Entirely from discussion chat:
    - chat_id = discussion chat id (-100xxxxxxxxxxx)
    - message_id = comment's id
    - thread_op_message_id = id of a channel post's duplicate in discussion channel
2. From both channel and discussion chat
    - chat_id = channel handle (@my_channel)
    - message_id = channel post's id
    - comment_message_id = comment's id **in discussion chat**
